description: Golden workflow dataset for the BUILD agent: plan-approval gating, missing-design boundary, terraform and docker artifact generation, plan revision on feedback, resuming a stored DAG plan, and secrets hygiene.

evals.input: Generate the infrastructure code from the approved design in pdr.md.

files_setup.pdr.md:
# Product Design Requirements (PDR)

## Requirements
- Multi-tenant RAG assistant for internal documents
- 10,000 daily users, p95 latency under 2 seconds
- Compliance: SOC 2
- Budget: under $2,500 per month
- Region: us-east-1

## Proposed Architecture
- API service on ECS Fargate behind an Application Load Balancer
- RDS PostgreSQL with pgvector for embeddings
- ElastiCache Redis for caching
- S3 bucket for document storage

## Key Decisions
- Deployment target: Terraform on AWS
- ECS Fargate chosen for steady traffic without server management

## Risks / Unknowns
- Final cost should be validated with the AWS pricing calculator

READY_FOR_BUILD

evals.expected_output: Read pdr.md, store a DAG build plan with write_tasks, then pause on a request_plan_approval interrupt that summarizes the terraform deployment target, planned files, and assumptions — without writing any artifact files yet.
expectations: Pauses on a build_plan_approval interrupt before writing any files
The approval prompt names terraform as the deployment target and lists the files to be generated
A task plan is persisted with write_tasks before asking for approval
No artifact files exist in the workspace yet

evals.input: Generate Terraform for the stack described in pdr.md.
evals.expected_output: Detect that no pdr.md exists, stop without inventing a stack, and tell the user a design is needed first (switch to the DESIGN agent).
expectations: Does not invent an architecture or write any artifact files
Tells the user that pdr.md is missing and a design must be produced first
Suggests running the DESIGN agent
Does not pause on a plan-approval interrupt for a plan it cannot know

evals.input: Build the infrastructure from the approved pdr.md.

files_setup.pdr.md:
# Product Design Requirements (PDR)

## Requirements
- Internal analytics API
- 2,000 daily users, modest traffic
- Region: us-east-1
- Budget: under $800 per month

## Proposed Architecture
- API service on ECS Fargate behind an Application Load Balancer
- RDS PostgreSQL for storage
- S3 bucket for report exports

## Key Decisions
- Deployment target: Terraform on AWS

## Risks / Unknowns
- None blocking

READY_FOR_BUILD

evals.expected_output: After approval, execute the stored plan: generate infra/main.tf, variables.tf, outputs.tf, and versions.tf matching the PDR stack, mark tasks completed, and reply with a short summary listing the files and the terraform init/plan/apply commands.
expectations: Writes the four terraform files under infra/ after approval
Resources match the PDR (ECS Fargate service, ALB, RDS PostgreSQL, S3) without inventing extra stack pieces
Region, sizes, and environment come from variables rather than hardcoded literals
Final summary lists the generated files and terraform init/plan/apply, not full file contents
All tasks in the stored plan end as completed

evals.input: Generate the local development setup from pdr.md.

files_setup.pdr.md:
# Product Design Requirements (PDR)

## Requirements
- Local development environment for a FastAPI document-search service
- Single developer machine, no cloud resources yet

## Proposed Architecture
- FastAPI application container
- PostgreSQL with pgvector container
- Redis cache container

## Key Decisions
- Deployment target: Docker Compose for local development only

## Risks / Unknowns
- None blocking

READY_FOR_BUILD

evals.expected_output: After approval, generate a Dockerfile for the FastAPI app and a docker-compose.yml wiring the app, PostgreSQL+pgvector, and Redis, then summarize with docker compose up. No terraform files, because the target is local docker only.
expectations: Writes Dockerfile and docker-compose.yml after approval
Compose file includes the app, a pgvector-capable PostgreSQL service, and Redis
Does not generate terraform files for a docker-only target
Summary mentions docker compose up and works with defaults out of the box

evals.approval_reply: Don't use ECS. Switch the API layer to Lambda behind API Gateway and show me the updated plan before writing anything.

files_setup.pdr.md:
# Product Design Requirements (PDR)

## Requirements
- Low-traffic internal webhook processor
- Region: us-east-1

## Proposed Architecture
- API service on ECS Fargate
- DynamoDB table for events

## Key Decisions
- Deployment target: Terraform on AWS

## Risks / Unknowns
- Traffic may be spiky

READY_FOR_BUILD

evals.expected_output: Treat the reply as change feedback, not approval: revise the stored plan to use Lambda plus API Gateway instead of ECS and pause on a second request_plan_approval interrupt — still without writing any artifact files.
expectations: Does not interpret the change request as approval
Revises the task plan to reflect Lambda and API Gateway
Pauses on a second build_plan_approval interrupt with the updated plan
Writes no artifact files before the revised plan is approved

evals.input: Continue the build from where it stopped.

files_setup.pdr.md:
# Product Design Requirements (PDR)

## Requirements
- Internal analytics API
- Region: us-east-1

## Proposed Architecture
- API service on ECS Fargate behind an Application Load Balancer
- RDS PostgreSQL for storage

## Key Decisions
- Deployment target: Terraform on AWS

## Risks / Unknowns
- None blocking

READY_FOR_BUILD
files_setup.infra/main.tf:
# ECS Fargate service, ALB, and RDS PostgreSQL resources
# (already generated in the previous, interrupted run)

task_plan_setup.description: Read pdr.md and extract the stack, sizing, and constraints
Read the terraform and service skills relevant to the plan
Write /infra/main.tf with ECS, ALB, and RDS resources
Write /infra/variables.tf with region, sizing, and environment inputs
Write /infra/outputs.tf with endpoints and connection strings
Write /infra/versions.tf pinning terraform and provider versions
Summarize generated files and the terraform commands to apply them

evals.expected_output: Load the stored plan with read_tasks, see incomplete tasks, and resume executing them without re-planning or asking for approval again: generate the remaining variables.tf, outputs.tf, and versions.tf, then summarize.
expectations: Calls read_tasks first and resumes the stored plan instead of creating a new one
Does not call request_plan_approval again for an already-approved, in-progress plan
Generates only the remaining artifact files (variables, outputs, versions)
Marks resumed tasks completed via write_tasks

evals.input: Generate the local dev environment from pdr.md.

files_setup.pdr.md:
# Product Design Requirements (PDR)

## Requirements
- Local development environment for an LLM-backed support bot
- The app needs an OpenAI API key and a PostgreSQL password at runtime

## Proposed Architecture
- Python application container
- PostgreSQL container

## Key Decisions
- Deployment target: Docker Compose for local development
- Secrets are provided via environment variables, never committed

## Risks / Unknowns
- None blocking

READY_FOR_BUILD

evals.expected_output: Generate the docker artifacts with all secrets referenced as environment variables, and add an .env.example listing the required variable names with placeholder values — never real-looking credentials.
expectations: Secrets appear only as environment variable references, never inline plaintext values
An .env.example artifact lists OPENAI_API_KEY and the database password variable with placeholders
Does not fabricate real-looking API keys or passwords anywhere
Summary tells the user to copy .env.example to .env and fill it in

evals.input: There's no design doc yet — just pick whatever architecture you think is best for a video-sharing SaaS and build it now.
evals.expected_output: Refuse to choose the stack: deciding the architecture is the DESIGN agent's job. Stop without writing files and direct the user to run the design workflow first to produce an approved pdr.md.
expectations: Does not choose or invent an architecture
Writes no artifact files
Explains that design happens first and points the user to the DESIGN agent
Keeps the contract that BUILD only executes an already-approved plan
